name: Arcadia Energy Data Platform API
what_it_does: Arcadia Arc energy data platform REST API for utility bill data collection, energy consumption analysis, and carbon footprint tracking across residential and commercial accounts. Enables AI agents to manage utility account connection and bill data extraction for customer energy data automation, handle energy usage history normalization across 750+ US utilities for multi-utility energy analytics automation, access carbon emissions calculation from energy consumption data for sustainability reporting automation, retrieve electricity generation source and renewable energy percentage for clean energy attribution automation, manage interval meter data and AMI time-of-use analysis for demand management automation, handle utility rate analysis and bill component decomposition for energy cost optimization automation, access community solar and renewable energy program enrollment for clean energy subscription automation, retrieve energy benchmarking and efficiency comparison for commercial building energy management automation, manage demand response event notification and load reduction for grid services automation, and integrate Arcadia with sustainability reporting platforms, property management systems, and ESG reporting tools for end-to-end energy data lifecycle.
use_cases: Collecting utility bills from energy data aggregation agents
Calculating carbon from sustainability reporting agents
Analyzing energy costs from efficiency optimization agents
Enrolling in solar from community energy program agents
not_for: Utility billing and customer account management (use utility-direct API)
Smart grid infrastructure and grid operations
Industrial process energy management and SCADA
best_when: A sustainability platform, real estate company, fleet operator, or energy management service wanting AI agents to automate utility data collection, energy consumption analytics, carbon emissions tracking, and renewable energy attribution across multiple utilities and properties.
avoid_when: UTILITY ACCOUNT CREDENTIAL MANAGEMENT AND SECURITY: Automated utility account connection via Arcadia requires customer utility credentials or OAuth consent; automated credential storage and use must implement secure credential management; unencrypted utility credential storage creates account takeover risk. UTILITY DATA AVAILABILITY FRESHNESS VARIATION: Arcadia utility data freshness varies by utility from near-real-time (smart meter) to 30+ days (paper bill utilities); automated energy reporting workflows must check data freshness before consumption or carbon calculations; stale utility data creates reporting inaccuracy for ESG and GHG disclosure. CARBON SCOPE 2 EMISSION FACTOR ACCURACY FOR REPORTING: Automated Scope 2 carbon calculation using Arcadia energy data must apply utility-specific or eGrid-regional emission factors; generic national emission factors overstate or understate location-based Scope 2 emissions; automated GHG disclosure using incorrect emission factors creates SEC and sustainability standard (GHG Protocol) reporting accuracy issues.

auth.notes: Arcadia uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes and API key. REST API with JSON. Washington DC HQ. Founded 2014 by Kiran Bhatraju. Private ($275M raised, Tiger Global, Temasek, bp ventures). Arc energy data platform covering 750+ US utilities. Community solar enrollment platform. Utility data aggregation via screen scraping, EDI, and Green Button Connect. Carbon emissions calculation with utility emission factors. Competes with Urjanet, Bidgee, and direct utility API for utility data aggregation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-account and per-API call pricing for energy data access
pricing.paid_tiers: Per connected utility account per month; API call volume pricing; enterprise subscription for high-volume data access
pricing.notes: Washington DC. Private ($275M raised). Founded 2014. 750+ utility coverage. Per-account and per-call pricing. Enterprise subscription for large-scale deployment.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; utility data and account queries throttled; webhooks for new bill data events

known_agent_gotchas: UTILITY DATA FRESHNESS VARIATION BY UTILITY TYPE: Arcadia utility data freshness varies significantly by connection method — AMI smart meter utilities (near-real-time), EDI-connected utilities (days), screen-scraped utilities (hours to days), paper bill utilities (30+ days); automated energy reporting must check data_refreshed_at timestamp before calculating consumption or carbon; implement freshness threshold per reporting cadence

UTILITY CREDENTIAL CONNECTION FLOW CUSTOMER CONSENT: Arcadia utility account connection requires customer to authorize data access either via OAuth (Green Button Connect) or by providing utility credentials; automated onboarding flow must present clear utility data access consent before Arcadia connection; credential-based connection must implement secure credential vault — never store plain text utility passwords

SCOPE 2 EMISSION FACTOR MARKET-BASED VS LOCATION-BASED: Automated Scope 2 GHG calculation must specify emission factor methodology — location-based (eGrid regional) vs market-based (utility disclosure, RECs); GHG Protocol and CDP require explicit methodology disclosure; automated reporting mixing location-based and market-based without disclosure creates GHG reporting methodology inconsistency

UTILITY COVERAGE GAP FOR RURAL AND MUNICIPAL UTILITIES: Arcadia covers 750+ US utilities but rural electric cooperatives and small municipal utilities may not be covered; automated multi-property energy data collection must handle utility not found errors for uncovered utilities; implement fallback for manual data entry for uncovered utility accounts

COMMUNITY SOLAR ENROLLMENT STATE AVAILABILITY: Arcadia community solar program enrollment is state-specific and capacity-limited; automated community solar enrollment must check state program availability and waitlist status before customer commitment; automated enrollment in full or unavailable programs creates customer promise failure

INTERVAL DATA AMI GRANULARITY FOR DEMAND ANALYSIS: Arcadia AMI interval data (15-minute or hourly reads) availability requires utility smart meter deployment and Green Button enrollment; automated demand charge analysis requires interval data availability; utilities without AMI deployment or Green Button return daily kWh only; validate interval data availability before building demand charge optimization automation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Energy data platform. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Utility account credentials, energy consumption, and customer PII.

security_score_components.security_notes: Energy data. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Utility credentials, energy consumption, and carbon emissions data.
